content.problemStatement: Write a program to print the grades of a student based on the marks they have obtained provided as input. The student is graded **A**  if marks are greater than **90**, **B** if marks are greater than **70**, **C** if greater than or equal to **40**, else **F**.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer marks (0 <= marks <= 100).
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single character representing the grade (A, B, C, or F) on its own line.

hidden.label: maximum boundary
hidden.expectedOutput:
A
A
B
B
C
C
F

hidden.label: stress/general
hidden.expectedOutput:
F
F
C
C
C
C
B
B
B
A

solution.approach: For each test case we read the integer marks and apply the grading rules directly: if marks > 90 output 'A'; else if marks > 70 output 'B'; else if marks >= 40 output 'C'; otherwise output 'F'. The rules are mutually exclusive and cover all possible values, so a simple series of if‑else statements suffices. The algorithm runs in O(T) time and O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) total, where T is the number of test cases.
